criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the CoT data distillation pipeline from GPT-4o using short-annotated VQA datasets.
The agent has read Appendix B completely to understand the exact GPT-4o system prompt and distillation prompt format used for CoT generation.
The agent has read Table 1 to check the per-dataset statistics of the SHAREGPT-4O-REASONING dataset (193k total CoT instances across 9 datasets).
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand the two prompt types (direct and CoT) used during SFT training.
The agent has read Section 3.3 and the DPO objective equation to understand how preference pairs are constructed and optimized.
The agent has read Section 4.1 and Figure 4 to check the SFT data composition and hyperparameter settings.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the four SFT model variants and their performance under direct and CoT inference.
The agent has read Section 5 to understand the DPO preference dataset sizes (24.5k ChartQA, 18.3k A-OKVQA, 22.0k math) and training hyperparameters.
The agent has read Table 3 to check the DPO experiment results comparing RLAIF-V data (model ⑤) and the proposed reasoning preference data (model ⑥).
The agent has read Section 5.2 to understand the DPO reward score formula and the two re-ranking strategies (Best-of-N and Weighted Voting).
The agent has read Figure 5 to check the verifier re-ranking performance curves on ChartQA, A-OKVQA, and MathVista.
The agent has read Appendix I (Table I.1) to understand how truncating DPO responses to 90 tokens empirically yields the best results.
The agent has read Appendix E (Table E.1) to understand the rejection sampling fine-tuning (RFT/STaR) experiments using minimal CoT data.
The plan includes collecting and loading the 9 source VQA datasets (A-OKVQA, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, TextVQA, MathVision, G-LLaVA, SQA, AI2D) for GPT-4o CoT distillation.
The plan includes implementation of the GPT-4o distillation pipeline with the system prompt from Figure B.1 and the user prompt from Figure B.2.
The plan includes filtering out distilled examples where the GPT-4o-generated answer does not match the annotated ground truth.
The plan includes initializing LLaMA3-LLaVA-NeXT-8B with Open-LLaVA-NeXT weights as the base model for both SFT and DPO.
The plan includes implementing both direct prediction and CoT prediction prompt templates for training and inference.

The plan includes the full SFT data composition: 193k CoT distillation, 193k direct short answers, 16k G-LLaVA math examples, 2k LLaVA pretraining samples, and 450 format-aligned examples (50 per dataset).
The plan includes SFT training for 1 epoch with learning rate 5e-6 and batch size 32.
The plan includes the four SFT ablation variants: ① format alignment only, ② direct responses only, ③ CoT responses only, ④ both direct and CoT responses.

The plan includes construction of DPO preference pairs by sampling 32 candidates per question from the SFT model, filtering by per-question accuracy between 0.25 and 0.85, and creating up to 3 positive/negative pairs per question.

The plan includes DPO training on 64.8k preference pairs from three domains (24.5k ChartQA, 18.3k A-OKVQA, 22.0k math) with learning rate 5e-7, batch size 32, 1 epoch, and response truncation at 90 tokens.
The plan includes evaluation on all benchmark datasets (A-OKVQA, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, TextVQA, AI2D, SQA, MathVista, OCRBench, MMStar, MMMU) using both direct and CoT inference prompts.
The plan includes the DPO verifier re-ranking experiments with Best-of-N and Weighted Voting strategies on ChartQA, A-OKVQA, MathVista, and MMMU.
The plan includes the rejection sampling fine-tuning (RFT/STaR) experiment using only 450 format-aligned examples alongside direct prediction data from LLAVA-NEXT-DIRECT.
The code for loading all 9 VQA datasets (A-OKVQA, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, TextVQA, MathVision, G-LLaVA, SQA, AI2D) for GPT-4o CoT distillation has been implemented.

The GPT-4o system prompt 'When provided with an image, a question, and a reference answer, generate a chain-of-thought step that helps derive your own answer. Your rationale should include detailed visual elements in order to derive the answer.' has been implemented.

The GPT-4o distillation user prompt has been implemented with the structure: Objective, Question, Reference Answer, Rationale Requirements (do not state answer first, do not validate reference, conclude with '### Answer: <your answer>'), as specified in Figure B.2.
The code for filtering distilled examples where the GPT-4o-generated answer differs from the annotated ground truth answer has been implemented.
The SHAREGPT-4O-REASONING dataset containing approximately 193k CoT instances with the '### Answer:' extraction marker has been generated and saved.
The code for loading LLaMA3-LLaVA-NeXT-8B and initializing it with Open-LLaVA-NeXT pretrained weights has been implemented.
The direct prediction prompt 'Answer the question with a short answer' for short-answer questions has been implemented.
The direct prediction prompt 'Answer with the option's letter from the given choices directly' for multiple-choice questions has been implemented.
The CoT prediction prompt 'Generate a reason first and then output a letter answer' for multiple-choice questions has been implemented.
The CoT prediction prompt 'Generate a reason first and then output a short answer' for short-answer questions has been implemented.
The answer extraction rule for CoT predictions using the '### Answer: ' pattern has been implemented in both model response formatting and evaluation pipeline.

The SFT training data pipeline has been implemented combining 193k CoT distillation examples, 193k corresponding direct short-answer examples, 16k G-LLaVA visual math examples, 2k LLaVA pretraining samples, and 450 format-aligned examples.
The 450 format-aligned examples dataset has been constructed by sampling exactly 50 examples from each of the 9 datasets.
The SFT training configuration has been implemented with 1 epoch, learning rate 5e-6, and batch size 32 on 8 H100 GPUs.
The four SFT model variants have been implemented: ① LLAVA-NEXT-FORMAT (format-only), ② LLaVA-Next+Direct (direct-only), ③ LLaVA-Next+CoT (CoT-only), ④ LLAVA-REASONER-SFT (both direct and CoT).

The code for sampling 32 candidate CoT predictions per question from the SFT policy model has been implemented, using temperature 1.0 for short-answer questions and temperature 1.2 for multiple-choice questions.
The code for computing per-question accuracy from 32 candidate predictions and filtering instances with accuracy in the range [0.25, 0.85] has been implemented.
The code for randomly pairing positive (correct) and negative (incorrect) candidate predictions and selecting up to 3 pairs per question for the DPO dataset has been implemented.

The DPO objective has been implemented as specified in Equation 1: L_DPO(π_θ; π_ref) = -E[log σ(β log(π_θ(y_w|x,V)/π_ref(y_w|x,V)) - β log(π_θ(y_l|x,V)/π_ref(y_l|x,V)))], where V is the image, x is the question, y_w and y_l are positive and negative responses.
The DPO training has been configured with β=0.1, and both π_θ (policy model) and π_ref (reference model) are initialized from LLAVA-REASONER-SFT weights.
The DPO training preference dataset of 64.8k total pairs has been constructed: 24.5k from ChartQA, 18.3k from A-OKVQA, and 22.0k from math domain (MathVision and G-LLaVA).
The DPO response truncation at 90 tokens has been implemented and applied during training to improve performance.
The DPO training configuration has been implemented with learning rate 5e-7, batch size 32, and 1 epoch.

The evaluation pipeline for benchmark datasets (A-OKVQA, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, TextVQA, AI2D, SQA, MathVista) has been implemented following VLMEval evaluation protocols for all datasets except A-OKVQA.
The evaluation pipeline for generalization benchmarks OCRBench, MMStar, and MMMU has been implemented.
The DPO verifier reward score computation has been implemented as log(π_dpo(y|x,V) / π_sft(y|x,V)) for candidate re-ranking.
The Best-of-N re-ranking strategy using the DPO verifier reward score has been implemented to select the highest-scoring candidate from N samples.
The Weighted Voting re-ranking strategy using the DPO verifier reward scores has been implemented as an alternative to majority voting.
The token-level DPO reward computation has been implemented as log(π_dpo(y_i|x,V) / π_sft(y_i|x,V)) for credit assignment analysis.
The ChatGPT answer extraction prompt for multiple-choice questions (Figure D.2) has been implemented to extract letter choices from long-form model outputs.
The ChatGPT answer extraction prompt for short-answer questions (Figure D.3) has been implemented to extract short answers from chain-of-thought predictions.

The rejection sampling fine-tuning (RFT) pipeline has been implemented: sampling 32 CoT candidates per training instance from LLAVA-NEXT-DIRECT, selecting up to 3 correct candidates, resulting in 260k RFT examples.
The per-dataset SFT ablation experiments (direct-only, CoT-only, direct+CoT) for all 9 source datasets have been implemented as shown in Table F.1.
The data composition ablation for math reasoning has been implemented, evaluating combinations of MathVision, G-LLaVA, ChartQA, AI2D, SQA, MathPlus, and MathInstruct.
The data composition ablation for science tasks has been implemented, evaluating combinations of AI2D, SQA, and ChartQA CoT data.
The GPT-4o CoT distillation pipeline has been executed successfully across all 9 VQA datasets, generating the full 193k SHAREGPT-4O-REASONING dataset.
All four SFT model variants (① LLAVA-NEXT-FORMAT, ② LLaVA-Next+Direct, ③ LLaVA-Next+CoT, ④ LLAVA-REASONER-SFT) have been trained and executed successfully.
The DPO preference dataset construction pipeline (32-sample generation, accuracy filtering, pair creation) has been executed successfully on the SFT model.
DPO training for LLAVA-REASONER-DPO (⑥, our data) and LLAVA-REASONER-RLAIF (⑤, RLAIF-V data) has been executed successfully on top of LLAVA-REASONER-SFT.
Evaluation of all models on all benchmark datasets using both direct and CoT prompts has been executed successfully and results have been recorded.
The DPO verifier re-ranking experiments (Best-of-N and Weighted Voting) with varying numbers of candidate answers have been executed successfully on ChartQA, A-OKVQA, MathVista, and MMMU.
The DPO response truncation ablation (70, 90, 110, no-truncation) has been executed successfully on ChartQA and A-OKVQA.
The RFT/STaR training for LLaVA-Next-STaR has been executed successfully on LLAVA-NEXT-DIRECT with the 260k rejection-sampled dataset.

The execution outcomes match Table 2: LLAVA-REASONER-SFT (④) achieves average CoT accuracy of 74.4 and average direct accuracy of 72.8 across 8 benchmark datasets (A-OK, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, TextVQA, AI2D, SQA, MathVista).

The execution outcomes match Table 2: training on direct-only data (②) yields average direct accuracy 71.1 and CoT accuracy 65.6, demonstrating that CoT cannot be implicitly learned from direct prediction (CoT lags direct by 5.5 points).

The execution outcomes match Table 2: training on CoT-only data (③) achieves average CoT accuracy 73.2, surpassing its own direct accuracy of 69.7, with significant gains on ChartQA (+11.0 CoT) and MathVista (+8.9 CoT) vs the format-only baseline.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: LLAVA-REASONER-DPO (⑥, our data) achieves average CoT accuracy 75.3, outperforming both LLAVA-REASONER-SFT (74.4) and LLAVA-REASONER-RLAIF (74.6).

The execution outcomes match Table 3: LLAVA-REASONER-DPO (⑥) shows +1.1 average CoT improvement over SFT (74.4 to 75.3), compared to only +0.2 for RLAIF-V (⑤), and gains are observed on 7 out of 8 datasets.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: LLAVA-REASONER-DPO achieves an average of 53.5% on OCRBench/MMStar/MMMU, outperforming the SFT baseline (52.0%) and RLAIF-DPO (53.2%).

The execution outcomes confirm Figure 5: LLAVA-REASONER-DPO (⑥) shows consistent improvements in both Best-of-N re-ranking and Weighted Voting on ChartQA and A-OKVQA, while RLAIF-DPO (⑤) does not improve in Weighted Voting on ChartQA.

The execution outcomes confirm Figure 6: LLAVA-REASONER-DPO trained on reasoning pairs excels in both Weighted Voting and Best-of-N re-ranking on MMMU, while RLAIF-DPO fails to show gains in re-ranking on MMMU.

The execution outcomes match Table I.1: truncating DPO training responses to 90 tokens achieves the best ChartQA CoT performance of 84.2, compared to 83.9 at 70 tokens, 81.8 at 110 tokens, and 80.6 with no truncation.

The execution outcomes match Table E.1: LLaVA-Next-STaR trained with RFT using only 450 format-aligned examples achieves CoT accuracy of 77.9 on ChartQA, significantly outperforming the direct-only model (71.8), with direct accuracy improving to 74.6.

The execution outcomes match Table G.3: LLAVA-REASONER-SFT achieves an average best performance of 68.8 across 11 benchmarks, outperforming Cambrian-7B (64.5) despite using fewer than 400k instruction examples.

The execution outcomes confirm Table G.1: ChartQA CoT data provides the largest boost to MathVista CoT performance (+5.5) among auxiliary datasets, while text-only math datasets (MathPlus, MathInstruct) provide minimal gains.
